title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Code Recall
summary: A delayed puzzle review reconstructs correctness, completeness, optimality, metrics, and selection from revision, configuration, seed, topology, frontier, predecessor map, state transition, draw record, accessibility report, profile, artifact, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for solver benchmark and decision report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Solver Selection
steps.instruction:
review case — A review puzzle team handles case 5742 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Solver Selection: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review puzzle team handles case 7015 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Solver Selection.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Solver Selection, keep Solver Selection connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Solver Selection, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Solver Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Solver Selection, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A review puzzle team handles case 7015 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose DFS, BFS, Dijkstra, or A-star from task, graph, evidence, latency, memory, pedagogy, and accessibility needs.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Solver Selection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: There is one universally best maze solver.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using choose and dijkstra plus specific case evidence.
